content: By transplanting the 2025 ICM intrinsic curiosity mechanism from AI autonomous agents into biological research, we can design "biological curiosity agents" that reward microbial populations for exploring novel metabolic niches instead of just optimizing for fixed resource intake, which could unlock new forms of synthetic adaptive microbial communities. This framework leverages the same core logic already applied to ICM-powered swarm UAVs, but redirects it to engineered biological agents defined in chemistry and biology.
